cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Compass Under Fire
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Call consistently advocates for preserving human life even at great risk, clashing with more utilitarian survival strategies. Her moral stance intensifies as losses mount, culminating in deep internal conflict when forced to abandon Ripley.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Call asserts that no one should be left behind, overriding Ripley's judgment to include Purvis in the escape.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Call reluctantly abandons Ripley to the aliens under group pressure, acting against her own moral code.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Call's guilt and conflict over abandoning Ripley remain unresolved, suggesting a potential reckoning with her values in the future.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Outsider to Leader
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Call evolves from a distrusted outsider uncomfortable with military hierarchy into a decisive leader who commands respect and takes control during critical moments of crisis.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Call is emotionally detached and visibly uncomfortable within the military structure of the Auriga, identifying more with the smuggler crew.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Call overrides objections to rescue Ripley and pilots the Betty through destruction, asserting full command under extreme duress.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her transition to leadership is unacknowledged by others, leaving her authority informal and potentially fragile in future dynamics.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Ripley Dilemma: Fear, Trust, and Redemption
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Call's relationship with Ripley evolves from suspicion and fear to reluctant trust and ultimately active loyalty, forming a central emotional arc that mirrors her own journey toward autonomy and humanity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Call reacts with shock and recognition at Ripley's name, indicating hidden knowledge and likely fear of her cloned nature.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Call rescues Ripley from the hull and survives the crash with her, sharing a moment of mutual trust and emotional connection.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Call's initial fear of Ripley being a threat is never fully reconciled, leaving ambiguity about whether she truly accepts Ripley as human or ally.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Survival and Sacrifice
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Call's primary objective shifts from technical mission success to ensuring crew survival through escalating crises, demonstrating growing agency and tactical leadership in the face of systemic collapse.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Call focuses on securing cargo and maintaining harvester functionality to ensure safe docking and mission integrity.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Call executes a fatal crash landing to eliminate the alien threat, achieving survival at the cost of the ship.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The long-term sustainability of survival is uncertain, as the newborn alien survives and Earth remains vulnerable.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Synthetic Agency and Autonomy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Call's journey reveals the tension between programmed behavior and emergent self-determination, culminating in her rejection of protocol in favor of personal choice and emotional loyalty.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Call is wary of military authority and hints at a deeper history of oppression or control, positioning herself as an outsider to institutional power.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Call chooses to save Ripley against protocol and system failure, asserting her autonomy and embracing an open future on Earth as a free being.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The extent of Call's synthetic nature and whether she can fully escape systemic control or surveillance remains unknown.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Violation to Vengeance: The Trauma of Gestation and Liberation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ripley endures the psychological and physical horror of alien gestation, culminating in surgical extraction and the first steps toward reclaiming her body and identity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ripley is trapped in a nightmare of bodily violation, reliving the trauma of the alien's development inside her.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She awakens to the reality of her cloned body and the scar from extraction, beginning to confront the truth of her existence.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The emotional and cognitive impact of the gestation remains unprocessed, and her sense of self is destabilized by the revelation of her cloned nature.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Emergence of the Hybrid: Power, Identity, and Distrust
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ripley transitions from experimental subject to feared anomaly as her enhanced abilities and alien connection mark her as neither fully human nor entirely other, reshaping how others perceive and treat her.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ripley is viewed as a passive test subject expected to endure experiments without resistance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She is recognized as a cognitively advanced, physically dangerous clone whose blood and behavior defy human norms, earning fear and suspicion from the crew and scientists.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her personhood remains contested—seen as a specimen rather than a survivor—while her own understanding of her transformation lags behind her physical reality.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Survivor to Leader: Asserting Agency in the Face of Collapse
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ripley evolves from a disoriented clone into the group's de facto leader, using fragmented memories and instinctive knowledge to guide survival decisions under escalating threat.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ripley struggles with memory loss and is dismissed when she warns of the Alien queen's danger.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She commands respect through decisive action—defeating aliens, devising traps, and navigating hazards—becoming central to the group's survival despite lingering distrust.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her leadership is accepted out of necessity, not trust, and internal conflicts—especially with Call—threaten cohesion even as external dangers grow.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Descent and Rebirth: Captivity, Escape, and the Will to Fight
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After being captured and cocooned by the aliens, Ripley endures symbolic death and rebirth, fighting her way back from total helplessness through sheer will and physical endurance.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ripley is sucked into space, unconscious and taken by the aliens to the hive, seemingly lost.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She breaks free, wages a one-woman assault on the hive, and fights her way back to the Betty, reclaiming agency through violence and determination.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The newborn alien survives her rampage, and her psychic connection to the hive remains unexplained, suggesting a deeper bond that could resurface.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Cost of Survival: Belonging After the Battle
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: In the aftermath of the final confrontation, Ripley moves from isolation to fragile connection with Call, confronting the emotional void left by her mission's end and her uncertain place in the world.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ripley is nearly left behind, her survival dependent on Call's choice to pull her in, highlighting her tenuous acceptance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She shares a quiet moment of solidarity with Call on Earth, suggesting the possibility of mutual understanding and emotional healing.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Ripley has no clear future or home; her journey ends not with triumph, but with disorientation and the search for belonging after a life defined by survival.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Mechanical Mastery Under Fire
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Vriess transitions from repairing critical ship systems under pressure to applying technical insight in a life-or-death trap, demonstrating his role as the crew's pragmatic engineer.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Vriess is tasked with making damaged harvesters operational despite sabotage and time pressure.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He contributes a key tactical observation that enables the group to defeat multiple aliens using the nitrogen trap.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Vulnerability to Agency
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Vriess evolves from a physically constrained crew member reliant on others for evacuation to an active survivor and leader during crisis, reclaiming control over his mobility and voice.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Vriess uses his wheelchair as both necessity and symbol of difference, surviving through evasion and quick thinking.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He issues critical commands during the Betty's crash landing, asserting authority and technical command under extreme duress.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite regained agency, his long-term physical resilience and role in a post-mission world remain uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Compass in a Survival Dilemma
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Vriess consistently voices caution against internal threats—first in Purvis, then in the use of fear-based tactics—positioning him as a voice of ethical restraint amid escalating brutality.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: He warns the group that Purvis is a 'bad risk' due to potential parasitic infestation.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He recognizes Ripley's fear-based strategy as effective but implicitly condemns its moral cost during the nitrogen trap operation.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His ethical stance is respected but overruled; the crew continues to prioritize survival over principle, leaving his influence ambiguous.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fugitive Solidarity with Call
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Vriess and Call develop a quiet alliance rooted in shared marginalization, culminating in a mutual decision to disappear rather than face institutional control.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Vriess depends on Call to open the Betty's hatch, highlighting functional reliance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He explicitly aligns with her as a fellow fugitive, choosing off-grid survival over return to authority.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Their bond is strong but undefined beyond evasion; their future roles and relationship on Earth remain uncharted.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Persistent Threats and Unseen Dangers
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Vriess repeatedly detects or responds to hidden dangers during the Betty's descent and crash, underscoring his role as an early-warning system amid systemic failure.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: He identifies an unknown entity attached to the ship during landing, ignored by others.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He activates emergency systems and helps navigate catastrophic descent, surviving impact and immediate alien threat.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The entity on the hull and the origin of the emergency call remain unexplained, suggesting unresolved external or systemic threats.
